message:
So my neighbor Mike showed up at our door during last week's outage with a rolling suitcase—literally a suitcase—packed with lanterns, battery packs, a hand-crank radio, backup batteries in three sizes, and what I'm pretty sure was a decade's supply of AA cells. I laughed at him for maybe thirty seconds before our Wi-Fi died and my phone hit 40%. Suddenly Mike looked like a genius.

He wasn't smug about it either. Just quietly handed me a USB power bank and asked if we wanted to charge our phones in shifts. Kid you know what? I felt genuinely grateful, and a little sheepish for all the times I'd teased him about being "that guy."

Turns out there's something really nice about knowing a few basic things will work when the power doesn't. Nothing extreme—just a flashlight or two that actually has batteries in it, maybe a portable charger tucked in a drawer, knowing where the manual can opener is. Mike's level of prep might be overkill for most of us, but his *vibe*—just matter-of-fact readiness—that's actually worth stealing.

I dug out my old lantern this week. It's still in one piece.

Do you keep a flashlight somewhere you'd actually find it in the dark?
